root_text.iti53:2.3: Sukhā vedanā, dukkhā vedanā, adukkhamasukhā vedanā.
root_text.iti53:2.7: Yato kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno sukhā vedanā dukkhato diṭṭhā hoti, dukkhā vedanā sallato diṭṭhā hoti, adukkhamasukhā vedanā aniccato diṭṭhā hoti;
root_text.iti53:2.8: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ‘bhikkhu ariyo, sammaddaso, acchecchi taṇhaṁ, vivattayi saṁyojanaṁ, sammā mānābhisamayā antamakāsi dukkhassā’”ti.
root_text.iti53:5.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti53:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti53:2.4: Pleasant feeling should be seen as suffering.
translation_text.iti53:2.5: Painful feeling should be seen as a dart.
translation_text.iti53:2.6: Neutral feeling should be seen as impermanent.
translation_text.iti53:2.7: When a mendicant has seen these three feelings in this way,
translation_text.iti53:2.8: they’re called a noble mendicant who sees rightly, has cut off craving, cast off the fetters, and by rightly comprehending conceit has made an end of suffering.”
translation_text.iti53:3.1: “A mendicant who sees pleasure as pain,
translation_text.iti53:4.2: And when freed in regards to that,
translation_text.iti53:4.3: that peaceful sage, with perfect insight,
translation_text.iti53:5.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

variant_text.iti53:2.8: acchecchi → acchejji (pts-vp-pli1); acchijji (mr) | vivattayi → vāvattayi (bj); vivaṭṭayi (pts-vp-pli1) | adda → ddakkhi (bj); addakkhi (sya-all); dakkhi (pts-vp-pli1, mr)
